key_insight: Verification is treated as a new scaling axis for LLMs: instead of only generating answers, an LLM can assign continuous, fine-grained correctness scores that improve selection and feedback.
engineering_relevance: This is useful for building more reliable agentic systems, because continuous verifier signals can rank candidates, monitor task progress, and provide dense rewards for RL without extra training.
method: Compute verifier scores by taking the expectation over scoring-token logits, yielding continuous rather than discrete judgments.
Improve verification by scaling score granularity, repeating evaluations, and decomposing criteria into simpler subchecks.
Use the continuous scores in a cost-efficient ranking algorithm to choose the best candidate among multiple solutions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports state-of-the-art results on Terminal-Bench V2 (86.5%), SWE-Bench Verified (78.2%), RoboRewardBench (87.4%), and MedAgentBench (73.3%).
watch_item: Because the method relies on probabilistic scoring and repeated/decomposed evaluation, performance may depend on prompt design, score calibration, and added inference cost.
